sourceMovie.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker (2019) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker clicked for you, Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker?
faq.answer: Try Guardians of the Galaxy, Star Wars, Star Wars: The Force Awakens—each shares DNA with Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker?
faq.answer: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker (2019) is best described as Adventure, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Star Wars Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
